pi_items.source: Confirmed via setup_task.sh which writes exactly 13.5 into assembly_instructions.txt.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which writes exactly 35.0 into assembly_instructions.txt.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which writes exactly 2.0 into assembly_instructions.txt.
Confirmed via setup_task.sh which writes exactly 5.0 into assembly_instructions.txt.
Confirmed via verifier.py logic where tolerance defaults to 0.5.

privileged_info_summary: I confirmed that the task operates on a real NASA conceptual aircraft model (eCRM-001), but attempts to create a synthetic reassembly scenario via setup_task.sh. The expected relative locations (Wing X=13.5, VTail X=35.0, HTail X=2.0, HTail Z=5.0) are explicitly defined in the assembly_instructions.txt generated by the setup script. However, I discovered multiple critical defects that make this task entirely unscorable: 1) setup_task.sh completely fails to zero out the original component translations because it incorrectly assumes XForm is a ParmContainer with a Name node. 2) verifier.py completely fails to find any components because it searches for <Name> as a direct child of <Geom>, missing them all. 3) Even if it found them, the verifier repeats the identical ParmContainer XForm mistake, meaning it would always evaluate the locations as 0.0 regardless of what the user inputs. 4) The required horizontal tail component doesnt even exist in the model; it is named Tail, so the verifiers substring check for horizontal will always fail.
